page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is an entry-level general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the N1 server family. It features 1 dedicated vCPU mapped to 1 physical core and 1 thread, running on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor clocked at 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB per core, and a 45 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results place the n1-standard-1 in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1088.79 is categorized as weak, while its multi-core performance falls into the bottom 10 percent of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block speeds at 10.68 GB/sec and write speeds at 9.71 GB/sec. Geekbench scores reflect this entry-level profile, yielding a single-core score of 895.0 and a multi-core score of 898.0.

Given its hardware profile, this instance is a cost-effective option for low-intensity, non-production, or lightweight utility workloads. It achieves 1.29 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 1.21 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, lightweight background tasks, and small-scale testing rather than resource-intensive database operations or data processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is an entry-level, general-purpose virtual machine within the N1 family. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor clocked at 2.3 GHz, it provides 1 dedicated vCPU, 1 physical core, and 4.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1088.79 and a Geekbench single-core score of 895.0. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 10.68 GB/sec for large block reads. This configuration is highly cost-effective for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development testing, and small utility tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is an entry-level general-purpose instance featuring 1 Intel Xeon vCPU, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is a 1-vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM general-purpose instance designed for lightweight utility and testing workloads.
tagline: An entry-level, cost-effective virtual machine with one dedicated core and four gigabytes of memory for basic utility workloads.
bullet_points: 1 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPU and 4.0 GB of RAM
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators
Factual bottom-tier CPU and memory benchmark performance
Suited for lightweight development, testing, and basic web serving
